us-politics.headline: Trump Presses Iran With Deadline, Threatens Total Destruction
us-politics.body:
Trump held a press conference doubling down on a Tuesday deadline for Iran to accept a nuclear deal, warning that the country 'could be taken out in one night' and threatening strikes on civilian infrastructure if no agreement is reached. He also suggested the US could charge tolls for Strait of Hormuz passage once it reopens, a move OPEC+ is already anticipating by agreeing to boost oil output. Trump invoked God's support for the US cause and brushed off questions about potential war crimes.

Critics across the spectrum are skeptical. Former National Security Advisor John Bolton argued that extending the deadline signals to Iran that Trump is not serious. A former NATO commander noted Iran still holds leverage. The administration did claim one win: a successful rescue operation of a missing F-15 airman downed in Iran.

sources.title: Trump says God supports U.S. cause in Iran war as he threatens wider bombing
5 takeaways from Trump’s news conference on Iran
Trump’s looming deadline for Iran
Trump suggests US could charge toll for Strait of Hormuz passage
Trump brushes off questions about potential war crimes in Iran

Trump doubles down on threats to Iran's civilian infrastructure unless there's a deal
Trump details rescue operation of missing F-15 airman in Iran: How it unfolded

Live updates: Trump warns all of Iran ‘could be taken out in one night’ as he sticks to deadline
Bolton: Trump extending deadline shows Iran ‘he’s not really serious’
OPEC+ agrees to boost output when Strait of Hormuz reopens

us-politics.headline: Trump Clashes With Supreme Court, Targets Birthright Citizenship
us-politics.body:
Trump publicly attacked the Supreme Court over its handling of birthright citizenship and tariff cases, signaling growing friction between the White House and the judiciary. The administration separately rescinded Title IX agreements with schools that had adopted gender identity policies, continuing its rollback of Biden-era rules. The EPA also took early steps to unwind methane flaring regulations.

These moves reflect a broader pattern of the administration using executive action to reverse policies across education, environment, and immigration. Supporters argue the changes restore legal clarity and reduce regulatory burden. Critics say they strip protections from vulnerable groups and undermine established legal frameworks.

sources.title: Trump rips Supreme Court over birthright citizenship, tariffs
Education Department rescinds previous Title IX agreements with schools over gender identity
EPA takes early step in methane rule rollback

us-politics.headline: 2026 Midterms Take Shape: Money, Primaries, and Party Battles
us-politics.body:
The 2026 midterm cycle is accelerating fast. A GOP super PAC announced a $342 million investment in Senate races, while an AI-backed super PAC is expanding into multiple Republican primaries after claiming early successes. In Montana, Senator Steve Daines has handpicked Kurt Alme as his preferred successor to hold the seat. Virginia Governor Glenn Youngkin hinted at future political ambitions, saying he has 'more to give.'

On the Democratic side, Virginia Governor Abigail Spanberger is facing a polling drop tied to gerrymandering controversy. Representative Anna Luna called on two House members - Tony Gonzales and Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick - to resign. The H2A farm worker visa debate is drawing opposition from both conservatives and liberals, a rare point of cross-partisan friction heading into the cycle.

sources.title: New poll reveals Spanberger's popularity is plummeting amid backlash over gerrymandering
AI-backed super PAC expands into multiple GOP primaries after claiming early wins
Youngkin on political future: ‘I have more to give’
Steve Daines' handpicked Senate successor Kurt Alme vows to keep Montana in Republican hands in 2026
GOP super PAC announces $342M investment in midterm Senate races
Luna says Gonzales, Cherfilus-McCormick ‘need to go’
Conservatives and liberals oppose H2A visa changes for immigrant farm workers

us-politics.headline: Hegseth, Mamdani, and Loomer Drive Culture War Headlines
us-politics.body:
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth cut military education ties with Ivy League institutions and redirected partnerships toward Hillsdale College, a conservative school, framing the move as a values-based reform. New York City mayoral candidate Zohran Mamdani unveiled a racial equity plan that drew an immediate pushback from the Department of Justice. Laura Loomer claimed credit for the arrest of a former Iranian commander's niece in California.

These stories share a common thread: political figures using high-profile actions to energize their respective bases ahead of elections and policy battles. Supporters of Hegseth's move call it a necessary correction to ideological bias in military training. Opponents say it politicizes the armed forces. The DOJ's swift response to Mamdani signals the federal government is watching local Democratic candidates closely.

sources.title: Hegseth cuts Ivy League ties in military education shake-up, taps Hillsdale
Mamdani unveils new 'racial equity plan' for more 'equitable future' that prompts quick DOJ pushback
Loomer takes credit for arrest of former Iranian commander’s niece in California

au-politics.headline: Trump blasts Australia over Iran war as fuel prices surge
au-politics.body:
Donald Trump publicly attacked Australia, Japan and South Korea for refusing to join US military operations against Iran, saying Australia 'didn't help' and threatening the country could be 'taken out tomorrow night.' The Albanese government has pushed back, with Communications Minister Michelle Rowland saying the government is focused on securing fuel supply and is 'not considering' rationing.

Iran has rejected a US truce offer, keeping the conflict live. The war is already hitting Australian households - PVC prices have skyrocketed due to supply chain disruption, and oil is trading at US$109 a barrel. The ASX opened higher despite the tension, tracking Wall Street gains.

sources.title: News live: Trump says Australia ‘didn’t help’ with Iran war; Rohan Dennis accuses media of ‘false narrative’ after wife’s death
Trump lashes out at Australia, Japan and South Korea for not helping in Iran war – video
Middle East war causes PVC prices to skyrocket
Live: Government focused on supply, 'not considering' fuel rationing: Rowland
Trump threatens 'demolition' of Iran and takes another swipe at Australia
Iran briefing with Matthew Doran: Iran rejects truce deal
Live: ASX opens higher after Wall St rises

au-politics.headline: Cyclone Maila threatens Queensland as reef damage mounts
au-politics.body:
Tropical Cyclone Maila is forecast to cross Far North Queensland, adding pressure to a coastline already reeling from environmental damage. At Ningaloo Reef in WA, a recent cyclone left animals strewn along beaches and raised fears about disruption to coral spawning season.

On the Great Barrier Reef, wrecked boats have been leaking styrofoam and chemicals for months, with salvage timelines drawing sharp criticism from environmental groups. The back-to-back incidents have renewed scrutiny of how quickly authorities respond to marine pollution events.

sources.title: Tropical Cyclone Maila likely to cross Far North Queensland
Fears for Ningaloo Reef after cyclone leaves animals strewn along beaches
Wrecked boats spill styrofoam, chemicals into Great Barrier Reef for months

au-politics.headline: Farmers and residents push back on renewable energy rollout
au-politics.body:
Farmers are demanding stronger protections in federal renewable energy policy, arguing landholders bear too much risk from wind and solar projects built on agricultural land. Industry groups want binding benefit-sharing agreements and clearer compensation rules before projects proceed.

In Tasmania, residents near a planned wind farm are raising a separate concern - that a link between the project and a state minister could weaken local advocacy. The minister has denied any conflict of interest. Both cases reflect growing community friction with the pace and governance of Australia's energy transition, a tension that cuts across traditional political lines.

sources.title: Farmers push for stronger protections in renewable energy policy
Residents worry link between minister and wind farm project could dilute advocacy

au-politics.headline: Army reservist charged; First Nations voters questioned at polls
au-politics.body:
A South Australian army reservist has been charged after allegedly working for the Ukrainian military without Defence Force authorisation, in what authorities describe as a breach of defence law. The case raises questions about how Australia manages reservists with ties to foreign conflicts.

Separately, First Nations voters in South Australia reported being asked to verify their identity at polling booths during the state's Voice to Parliament vote, prompting calls for a formal review. Advocates say the questioning was discriminatory and inconsistent with how other voters were treated. The SA government has not yet committed to an inquiry.

sources.title: Army reservist allegedly worked for Ukraine military without authorisation
'Are you sure?': First Nations voters asked about identity at polling booths

world.headline: US-Iran War Escalates as Hormuz Deadline Looms
world.body:
The United States and Israel are intensifying strikes on Iran, hitting targets including the Bushehr nuclear plant and a top university, as Trump's deadline for Iran to surrender control of the Strait of Hormuz approaches. Iran shows no sign of compliance, and has instead pushed a counter-proposal to end the war. Trump has threatened to destroy Iran in a single night if no deal is reached.

Hezbollah and the Houthis have joined Iran in striking Israel, widening the conflict across the region. US Defense Secretary Hegseth confirmed strikes are increasing. Trump separately claimed Iranians are begging the US to continue bombing - a statement dismissed by critics. Democrats accused Trump of threatening war crimes; most Republicans backed the approach.

A US airman was rescued from a remote part of Iran amid the fighting. Trump threatened to jail a reporter who leaked details of the operation.

sources.title: Trump's Hormuz ultimatum nears - with little indication Iran is on board
Trump threatens to take out Iran in 'one night' if no deal before deadline
How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
Trump says US could charge for Strait of Hormuz passage amid Iran war

Trump warns Hormuz deadline ‘final’ as Iran pushes proposal to end war

world.headline: Gaza and Lebanon Bloodshed Continues as Protests Erupt
world.body:
Israeli strikes killed at least ten Palestinians near a Gaza school, with additional deaths reported in clashes between Hamas and a militia. Israel has also deepened its ground offensive in Lebanon, with renewed airstrikes pounding the country as Hezbollah simultaneously fires on Israeli territory.

Israeli soldiers fired tear gas at Palestinian youth during a raid in Hebron. In Tel Aviv, protesters swarmed the US embassy demanding an end to the wars. The overlapping fronts - Gaza, Lebanon, and now Iran - are stretching the region toward a broader conflagration.

sources.title: Protesters swarm US embassy in Tel Aviv, demanding end of wars
Israeli soldiers fire tear gas at Palestinian youth during raid in Hebron
Israeli air strike kills at least 10 Palestinians near Gaza school
Renewed Israeli strikes pound Lebanon as ground offensive deepens
Ten killed in Israeli strikes and clashes between Hamas and militia in Gaza, local sources say

world.headline: Oil Crisis Bites as Global Energy Prices Soar
world.body:
The conflict around the Strait of Hormuz - through which roughly a fifth of the world's oil passes - is driving a sharp global energy crisis. Trump floated the idea of charging ships for passage through the strait if the US takes control, a move that would reshape global trade. Asia has emerged as the region most exposed to the disruption.

The effects are already visible. Cairo has begun dimming street lights as energy costs surge. Analysts warn that a prolonged blockade or military escalation in the Gulf could push prices far higher.

sources.title: Trump says US could charge for Strait of Hormuz passage amid Iran war
How Asia became ground zero for the oil crisis
Cairo faces darker nights as global energy prices continue to soar

world.headline: Russia Jails Kursk Governor; Ukraine Strikes Kill Child
world.body:
Russia sentenced the former governor of the Kursk region to prison in a corruption case linked to Ukraine's cross-border incursion last year. The prosecution signals Moscow's continued effort to assign blame for the military embarrassment that saw Ukrainian forces briefly hold Russian territory.

Meanwhile, Russia ramped up strikes on Ukraine, with rescuers pulling a child from rubble after a residential building was hit. The strikes come as international attention remains heavily focused on the Middle East, reducing pressure on both sides to negotiate.

sources.title: Russia jails former Kursk governor in Ukraine incursion-linked graft probe
Child rescued from rubble after Russia ramps up strikes on Ukraine

ai.headline: OpenAI's Leadership Crisis Deepens Amid Structural Upheaval
ai.body:
OpenAI insiders are openly questioning Sam Altman's trustworthiness, with sources describing a culture of opacity and misaligned incentives at the top. The criticism lands as the company pushes an ambitious economic vision - public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day workweek - that some see as a distraction from governance problems inside the building.

Meanwhile, a group of OpenAI alumni have quietly assembled a fund approaching $100 million to back early-stage AI startups, signaling that the talent exodus is becoming a capital exodus too. Iran has also threatened OpenAI's Stargate data center in Abu Dhabi, adding geopolitical pressure to an already turbulent moment for the company.

sources.title: “The problem is Sam Altman”: OpenAI Insiders don’t trust CEO
OpenAI’s vision for the AI economy: public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day workweek
OpenAI alums have been quietly investing from a new, potentially $100M fund
Iran threatens ‘Stargate’ AI data centers
Iran threatens OpenAI’s Stargate data center in Abu Dhabi

ai.headline: Anthropic Bets Big on Compute as AI Infrastructure Race Accelerates
ai.body:
Anthropic has expanded its partnership with Google and Broadcom to secure multiple gigawatts of compute capacity, a move that underscores how the AI arms race is increasingly fought at the infrastructure layer. Gigawatt-scale deals were unthinkable just a few years ago. Now they are table stakes.

Gary Marcus, meanwhile, has published a sharp takedown of the first company to claim an $1.8 billion AI valuation, arguing the figure obscures thin fundamentals behind aggressive marketing. Taken together, the two stories capture a market where capital is flooding in faster than scrutiny can follow.

sources.title: Anthropic expands partnership w Google and Broadcom for multiple GW of compute
The back story behind the first "$1.8B" dollar "AI Company"

ai.headline: Robotics AI Hits 99% Reliability in Real Production Tasks
ai.body:
Generalist's GEN-1 robotics model has reached 99% task reliability across jobs as varied as folding boxes and repairing vacuums, a threshold that matters because production environments have zero tolerance for failure rates that research demos routinely ignore. The result suggests general-purpose physical AI is closer to commercial deployment than most timelines assumed.

Separately, MIT Technology Review argues that granular task-level data - not broad employment statistics - is the only honest way to measure how AI is actually reshaping work. The robotics milestone makes that data more urgent than ever.

sources.title: From folding boxes to fixing vacuums, GEN-1 robotics model hits 99% reliability
The one piece of data that could actually shed light on your job and AI

ai.headline: AI Floods iTunes Charts While Google Ships Quiet Offline Dictation
ai.body:
An AI-generated singer named Eddie Dalton now holds eleven spots on the iTunes singles chart, despite being neither human nor real. The chart takeover is less a novelty than a stress test - one that exposes how unprepared music platforms are for synthetic content at scale.

On the tools side, Google quietly released an offline-first AI dictation app for iOS, and a developer shipped Ghost Pepper, a local hold-to-talk speech-to-text tool for macOS. Both arrive without fanfare. The pattern is consistent: capable AI voice tools are shipping faster than any policy framework can track them.

sources.title: AI singer now occupies eleven spots on iTunes singles chart
Google quietly launched an AI dictation app that works offline
Show HN: Ghost Pepper – Local hold-to-talk speech-to-text for macOS

btc.headline: Bitcoin Climbs Past $70K but Options Market Signals Caution
btc.body:
Bitcoin pushed back above $70,000 Monday as contrarian bottoming signals emerged, but profit-taking is keeping a lid on the rally. Analysts point to Trump's ultimatum to Iran as a potential geopolitical catalyst, though its staying power remains uncertain.

The options market tells a more cautious story. Traders are quietly pricing in significant downside risk, suggesting institutional hedging activity beneath the surface optimism. Strategy added another $330 million in Bitcoin last week, absorbing nearly three times the new supply mined - a dynamic some analysts say could push BTC toward $110,000 if demand holds. The paper losses on Strategy's holdings topped $14.5 billion in Q1.

sources.title: Bitcoin may hit $110K as Strategy absorbs nearly 3x new BTC supply
Bitcoin options market is quietly pricing a major downside move
Can Trump’s ultimatum to Iran push Bitcoin price back up to $75K?
Bitcoin climbs above $70,000 as more contrarian bottoming signs emerge
Bitcoin profit taking keeps BTC below $70K as Trump doubles down on Iran
Strategy adds $330M BTC as paper losses top $14.5B in Q1

btc.headline: Polymarket Overhauls Exchange, Drops Bridged USDC
btc.body:
Polymarket is executing a full exchange upgrade, replacing the bridged USDC.e token with a native USDC-backed instrument and taking direct control of its own trading infrastructure and resolution mechanisms. The move signals a push for greater autonomy and reduced reliance on third-party token bridges.

The upgrade comes as prediction markets face mounting regulatory scrutiny. A US appeals court separately upheld a block on New Jersey's attempt to shut down Kalshi's sports event contracts, handing the CFTC-regulated platform a significant legal win over state-level enforcement. The ruling reinforces federal jurisdiction over prediction markets and could shape how platforms like Polymarket navigate US regulatory exposure going forward.

sources.title: Polymarket drops USDC.e for USDC-backed token in exchange overhaul
US appeals court upholds preventing New Jersey enforcement against Kalshi
Polymarket reveals a 'full exchange upgrade' to take control of its own trading and truth
Appeals court blocks New Jersey from shutting down Kalshi's sports markets

btc.headline: Dimon Warns JPMorgan Must Move Faster on Tokenization
btc.body:
Jamie Dimon used JPMorgan's annual shareholder letter to flag blockchain and stablecoins as sources of genuine competitive threat, naming new entrants rather than legacy rivals as the bank's primary concern. He said JPMorgan must accelerate its tokenization efforts through its Kinexys platform or risk falling behind.

The comments land as tokenization gains real traction. Bernstein analysts argued this week that Figure Technology's tokenized credit platform could double its stock price through accelerated loan growth. Bitmine also disclosed its Ethereum treasury has reached 4.8 million ETH ahead of a move to NYSE listing. Institutional infrastructure is building fast.

sources.title: JPMorgan's Jamie Dimon sees ‘new competitors’ from blockchain,  stablecoins
Figure Technology's tokenized credit platform could help double stock price: Bernstein
Jamie Dimon says JPMorgan must move faster as tokenization reshapes finance
Bitmine's ether treasury hits 4.8 million ETH as stock listing moves to NYSE

btc.headline: Aave Loses Risk Manager as DeFi Governance Strains Show
btc.body:
Chaos Labs is exiting its role as Aave's primary risk manager following disputes with contributors and a broader exodus of key participants from the protocol's governance structure. The departure leaves one of DeFi's largest lending platforms without its main risk oversight function at a sensitive moment for the sector.

The split highlights a recurring tension in decentralized protocols - how to retain specialized contributors when governance disagreements escalate and compensation structures are subject to community votes. Aave will need to move quickly to replace the risk management function, particularly as crypto markets remain volatile and lending protocols face ongoing liquidation pressure.

sources.title: Aave loses key risk manager, Chaos Labs, amid contributor exodus and disputes

food.name: Miso-Glazed Black Cod
food.description: Silky, buttery cod fillets marinated in white miso, mirin, and sake then broiled until caramelised and lacquered
food.tip: Marinate for at least 24 hours — the longer the fish sits, the deeper the umami penetration and the better the glaze caramelises under the broiler

exercise.name: The Deck of Cards Workout
exercise.equipment: A standard deck of playing cards, exercise mat
exercise.description: Assign a bodyweight exercise to each suit, flip cards one by one, and perform reps matching the card's number — the randomness keeps your heart rate unpredictable and your mind fully engaged

learn.topic: The Ship of Theseus and Personal Identity
learn.description: This ancient Greek paradox asks whether an object that has had all its components replaced remains fundamentally the same object — and when applied to humans, whose cells, beliefs, and memories constantly change, it becomes a genuinely unsettling question about what 'you' actually are
learn.why: Philosophers from Heraclitus to Derek Parfit have used it to challenge assumptions about continuity and selfhood, and spending an hour tracing it from Greek mythology through Locke's memory theory to modern neuroscience will quietly rewire how you think about identity, change, and personal responsibility
